data.description:
## Why
list-epics duplicates query/reduction logic that belongs in the generic list primitive. Generalize the generic filters so 'get epics' is one generic call, then reduce list-epics to a thin deprecating wrapper. Orthogonal to the bash->Python strangler-fig (adult-oxide-slave) — tracked on its own.

## What
1. Expand the generic 'list' read primitive (rebar._engine_support.reads.list_states + ticket_reads CLI + library list_tickets + MCP list tool):
   - --min-children=N : tickets with >= N non-deleted direct children.
   - --unblocked / --blocked : filter by blocking state (reuse rebar.graph find_ready_tickets / all_blockers_closed).
   - include a children_count field in list output (cheap, additive, universal) so one call returns enriched epics.
2. list-epics becomes a thin wrapper: (a) display deprecation warning (stderr), (b) ONE call to get epics (generic list --type=epic --status=open,in_progress [--unblocked] [--min-children]), (c) ONE call to get P0 bugs (generic list --type=bug --priority=0, counted), (d) assemble the {p0_bugs, epics} output. Replaces the bespoke ticket-list-epics.sh reduction.
3. Surfaces: CLI dispatcher arm, library list_epics(), MCP list_epics tool — all wrap + warn.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] generic list gains --min-children + --unblocked/--blocked + children_count, at CLI + library + MCP
- [ ] new filters/field covered by tests; list output schema updated for children_count
- [ ] list-epics is a wrapper: warns + 1 call epics + 1 call P0 bugs -> {p0_bugs, epics}; no bespoke reduction
- [ ] deprecation warning at CLI (stderr, not stdout/JSON) + library (DeprecationWarning) + MCP description
- [ ] list-epics output parity preserved ({p0_bugs, epics}); existing schema/MCP tests green or updated
- [ ] docs note the deprecation + new generic filters
data.title: Generalize list filters + deprecate list-epics to a thin wrapper (orthogonal to strangler-fig)
